ux_issues.description: GetChatHistory shows 11 messages but ListChatSessions reports messageCount=27. The discrepancy is likely because tool_call and tool_result messages are counted in messageCount but not returned in GetChatHistory. The user sees a session with '27 messages' but only 11 are retrievable, which could be confusing.

Some assistant messages appear to be missing from GetChatHistory for planning mode messages 3-5 (family activities, driving logistics, wildlife watching). User messages are present but their corresponding assistant responses are not persisted. This means chat history replay would show user questions without answers.

Day 4 (drive to Monteverde), Day 6 (night walk only), Day 8 (park only), and Day 10 (departure) each have only 1 activity. Days 4 and 10 are understandable as travel days, but Days 6 and 8 feel sparse. The AI could have proactively added more activities for those days.

ai_behavior_issues.description: The AI made 3 web_search calls that all failed (no web access in local env) before falling back to knowledge. While this is expected in local dev, it wasted tool loop iterations. The AI could be prompted to detect the 'no_web_access' status more efficiently and skip further web searches.

tool_failures.description: All web_search calls returned 'no_web_access' status (expected in local test environment). Not a bug — web search is not configured locally.

usefulness_evaluation.trip_creation_notes: Trip created immediately from natural language description. Title, description, and destination country all correct. create_trip tool called proactively.
usefulness_evaluation.itinerary_quality_notes: Excellent 10-day itinerary covering all 3 locations with age-appropriate activities. Frog tours for Noah, swimming for Emma, safety tips throughout. Some days are sparse (1 item). Total 18 items across 10 days is solid.
usefulness_evaluation.persona_handoff_notes: Expert handoff to 'Mateo' (Costa Rican wildlife and family expeditions) triggered naturally when asking about kid activities. The expert's responses were deeply knowledgeable and family-focused.
usefulness_evaluation.booking_parsing_notes: No booking ingestion tested in this persona (not part of test plan).
usefulness_evaluation.companion_mode_notes: Companion mode responses were concise, practical, and location-aware. The 'what to do today' response was perfect for a tired family arriving — suggested a soda for lunch and hot springs. Water safety advice was specific and actionable.
usefulness_evaluation.overall_notes: Strong performance across all test vectors. The AI demonstrated excellent context retention (remembering kids' names, ages, interests across messages), proactive safety advice, and good use of tools. Minor issues with chat history persistence and some sparse itinerary days.
usefulness_evaluation.narrative: David Chen's family trip planning went smoothly. The AI immediately understood the family context and created an age-appropriate, safety-conscious itinerary. The expert handoff to Mateo was seamless and provided genuinely useful Costa Rica-specific knowledge about frog tours, snorkeling safety, and medical facilities. Companion mode showed excellent brevity and practical advice. The full trip lifecycle (selection -> planning -> active -> companion -> sharing -> completion) worked end-to-end without errors.
